topics: Basic concepts of Thermodynamics - Microscopic & macroscopic approach
Quasi-static process. Concepts of heat and work transfer- displacement work and other modes of work. Zeroth law of Thermodynamics- concept of temperature and thermal equilibrium
Various thermometers. First law of thermodynamics- Concept of energy and various forms of energy- internal energy
specific heats. First law applied to a closed system undergoing change of state and a cycle
Steady flow energy equation- applied to steady flow engineering devices- turbines
throttling process - Derivation and simple problems
Limitations of first law of thermodynamics
Kelvin-Planck and Clausius statements and their equivalence
thermodynamic temperature scale. Concept of entropy
Principle of increase of entropy. Availability and irreversibility
concept of exergy. Air standard cycles
Refrigeration effect & C.O.P Refrigerants- Classification of refrigerants
desirable properties of refrigerants. Refrigeration cycles - Reversed Carnot cycle
representation on T-S and P-H diagrams
simple problems. Compressors - Reciprocating compressors- Principle of operation
minimum work condition for multi-stage compression. -simple problems
Rotary compressors- mechanical details and working principle (description only
Gas turbines - Classification, Thermodynamic analysis of gas turbine cycles-open, closed and semi closed cycle

ideal working cycle- Brayton cycle-P-V and T-S diagram, thermal efficiency. Effect of compressor and turbine efficiencies. Comparison of gas turbine and IC engines, Analysis of open cycle gas turbine, Improvements of the basic gas turbine cycles-regeneration, intercooling and reheating- cycle efficiency and work output. . Combustion chambers for gas turbines. Fuels and Combustion- Types of fuels, Properties of fuels, Combustion equations, Combustion analysis by mass and volume - Conversion of gravimetric to volumetric analysis - Conversion of volumetric to gravimetric analysis, Analysis of fuel combustion-Air fuel ratio, equivalence ratio, minimum quantity of air, flue gas analysis, excess air
